what: Pure MXFP4 kernel: use MXFP4 data for both K (dot_scaled) and V (manual fp4 dequant). Load MXFP4 packed data once, use full 576 dims for K via dot_scaled, and extract first 512 dims for V via manual fp4->bf16 dequant with block-32 e8m0 scales. Saves ~29% bandwidth vs loading separate FP8 V.
why: Current kernel loads 306B MXFP4 for K + 512B FP8 for V = 818B per token. Using MXFP4 for V only needs 306B total (data already loaded for K) + 272B for V-only portion. Decode is bandwidth-bound, so reducing memory traffic directly improves latency.
